package_concept: continuity-fix temple architecture interaction: exterior veranda / column corridor edge start to wet stairs to eaves/roof-edge endpoint with controlled local breakage
route_summary: exterior column corridor pressure -> wet stone stairs -> eaves/roof-edge endpoint
continuity_source: SHOT-03-V001 mixed-positive but continuity issue; V002 fixes exterior start and route connective tissue

references.label: @FENSHOU_UPLOADSAFE
references.duty: Fenshou identity only. Black/red armored male warrior, face, hair, armor, silhouette, body type, aggressive forward pressure.

references.label: @SHUANGJI_UPLOADSAFE
references.duty: Highest-priority Shuangji female identity protection only. Female face, female identity, silver-blue high ponytail, blue-silver armor collar/shoulders, white-blue robe panels. Do not use as action/rhythm/scene/camera reference.

references.label: @TEMPLE_SCENE_UPLOADSAFE
references.duty: Rainy ancient temple exterior/veranda world only: frontal main-hall axis, exterior corridor, wet stone floor, wet stairs, railings, eaves, roofline, door/window lattice, cold gray-blue rainy mood. Do not use as character identity reference.

route_timing_design.beat: exterior veranda / column corridor edge start
requirements: begin outside or semi-outside, not deep inside the hall
courtyard, rain, exterior corridor edge, column line, eaves, and railing should be readable
Fenshou and Shuangji are already fighting from SHOT-02 continuity
columns and railing shape the movement
one fighter drives the other sideways along the exterior column line
hard brief contact, rebound, and visible body reaction
fight moves naturally onto wet stone steps
step edges and height difference remain readable
show traction, foot skid, slip recovery, pivot, and forced sidestep
one fighter nearly loses footing, catches balance, and counters
railing or wooden panel may crack from body contact

route_timing_design.beat: eaves / roof-edge endpoint with controlled local damage
requirements: fighters reach eaves or roof-edge area
roofline, tiles, wooden eaves, railing, or door/window lattice are visible
a few tiles slide or scatter
eaves wood splinters or lantern/banner is knocked aside
ending implies SHOT-04 structural escalation but does not perform it
no full temple collapse, no full roof duel, no snow transition

controlled_local_breakage_allowed: a few roof tiles slide or scatter
lantern or banner gets knocked aside
rainwater and splinters react to impact
action_language_requirements: defender reaction through torso displacement, guard buckle, shoulder jolt, forced sidestep, foot skid, or recoil
tactical use of columns, railing, wet steps, eaves, tiles, and wood panels
footwork must show traction, step edge, slip, recovery, and pivot
environmental response must be local and controlled
camera_requirements: do not open deep inside the hall
keep courtyard / exterior veranda / column corridor edge readable
use column line, railing, wet step edge, eaves, roofline, and door/window lattice as clear architectural anchors
lateral tracking along the exterior column corridor is allowed
slight rising or diagonal follow during stairs is allowed only if feet remain grounded and visible
avoid excessive shake that hides contact
avoid occlusion that hides the actual contact point too long
avoid sudden teleport from stairs to roof/eaves; show connective movement
identity_requirements: Fenshou and Shuangji must remain visually distinct
result_note: No-submit package only; human review required before any live submit authorization.
